members.member: The default base type assigned to a new Orchestration. This is the type the script extends unless changed.
The default format used when saving Orchestration [Resource]s. [code]Text[/code] is human-readable and version-control friendly; [code]Binary[/code] is more compact.
When enabled, the updater also reports preview (pre-release) builds in addition to stable releases.
When set to [code]true[/code], new dynamic and promotable operator nodes are available in the action dialog menu. When set to [code]false[/code], the old non-dynamic operator nodes are available.
Interval, in seconds, at which open Orchestrations are automatically saved. Set to [code]0[/code] to disable autosave.
If [code]true[/code], reopens orchestrations that were open in the last session when the editor is reopened on a given project.
Automatically reload open Orchestrations when their files are changed on disk outside the editor.
Reload and re-parse Orchestrations on save so validation always reflects the latest changes.
Highlight, in the script list, Orchestrations attached to the currently edited scene.
Determines how the script list is ordered: unsorted, by name, or by path.
Determines how entries are labeled in the script list: by name, by parent directory and name, or by full path.
Delay, in seconds, after editing stops before an Orchestration is re-parsed for validation.
Idle re-parse delay used while an Orchestration currently has validation errors; typically shorter for quicker feedback.
Open the All Actions search menu centered on the mouse cursor.
Automatically close the All Actions search menu when it loses focus.
When ranking action search results, prefer property accessors over equivalent getter/setter methods.
Display human-friendly names for functions in the components panel instead of their raw identifiers.
Display human-friendly names for graphs in the components panel instead of their raw identifiers.
Prompt for confirmation before deleting nodes from a graph.
When dragging a node, automatically break its execution (control flow) connections.
Show the background grid in the graph editor.
The pattern used to draw the graph background grid: [code]Dots[/code] or [code]Lines[/code].
Snap nodes to the grid when moving them in the graph editor.
Include advanced, developer-oriented details in graph node and pin tooltips.
When a dragged connection could be autowired in more than one way, show a dialog to choose; otherwise connect automatically.
Show the minimap overlay in the graph editor.
Show the arrange-nodes button in the graph editor toolbar.
Show contextual action hints overlaid on the graph editor.
Scales the clickable area around pins when making connections, making them easier to target.
Highlight the connections attached to selected nodes.
Make newly created graph nodes resizable by default.
Automatically size graph nodes to fit their content.
Show type icons next to pins on graph nodes.
Maximum call-stack depth allowed at runtime before a stack-overflow error is reported.
Maximum number of iterations a loop node may run before execution is aborted, guarding against infinite loops.
Always record call-stack information at runtime, even when the debugger is not attached. May affect performance.
Always record local variable values at runtime, even when the debugger is not attached. May affect performance.
Enable node convergence, allowing multiple execution paths to merge at a single node.
Master toggle for Orchestration validation warnings. When disabled, the individual warnings below are not reported.
Emitted when a variable is used before being assigned a value.
Emitted when a variable is modified with a compound assignment operator before being initialized.
Emitted when a local variable is declared but never used.
Emitted when a local constant is declared but never used.
Emitted when a class variable is declared but never used.
Emitted when a function parameter is never used.
Emitted when a signal is declared but never explicitly used.
Emitted when a local declaration shadows another declaration in the current class.
Emitted when a local declaration shadows a declaration in a base class.
Emitted when a declaration has the same name as a global type or constant.
Emitted for code that can never be executed, such as statements after a return.
Emitted for a match pattern that can never be reached.
Emitted for an expression whose result is discarded and has no effect.
Emitted for a ternary expression whose result is discarded.
Emitted when the two branches of a ternary produce incompatible types.
Emitted for a declaration that has no static type.
Emitted for a declaration whose static type is inferred rather than explicit.
Emitted when accessing a property that may not exist on the inferred type.
Emitted when calling a method that may not exist on the inferred type.
Emitted for an unsafe cast from [Variant] to a specific type.
Emitted when a supertype is passed where a subtype argument is required.
Emitted when a void function returns a call whose void result cannot be guaranteed.
Emitted when a function's return value is discarded.
Emitted when a static function is called from an instance instead of the type.
Emitted when a class should be marked as a tool but is not.
Emitted for a redundant static-unload annotation.
Emitted when awaiting an expression that is not a coroutine or signal.
Emitted when a coroutine or signal is used without awaiting it.
Emitted when an assert condition is always true.
Emitted when an assert condition is always false.
Emitted when integer division discards the remainder.
Emitted for an implicit float-to-integer narrowing conversion.
Emitted when an integer is assigned to an enum without an explicit cast.
Emitted when casting an integer to an enum with no matching member value.
Emitted when an enum-typed variable has no explicit default value.
Emitted when a script file is empty.
Emitted for use of a deprecated keyword.
Emitted for identifiers containing misleading or confusable characters.
Emitted when a local declaration shadows one declared later in the block.
Emitted when an identifier will be shadowed later in the block.
Emitted when reassigning a lambda capture, which does not modify the outer variable.
Emitted when a type is inferred from a [Variant] value.
Emitted when a method overrides a native method.
Emitted when a get-node default is used on a member that is not marked on-ready.
Emitted when the on-ready and export annotations are used together.
Emitted when a property is called as if it were a function.
Emitted when a constant is used as if it were a function.
Emitted when a function is accessed as if it were a property.
The [PackedScene] instantiated to present dialogue messages at runtime.
Specifies the [CanvasLayer] layer to be applied to the Print String Overlay HuD.
Scale applied to the on-screen overlay used by Print String nodes.
The active color theme preset for graph nodes and connections. Choosing [code]Custom[/code] lets the individual colors below take effect.
Color used for execution (control flow) connections in the graph.
Color used for Any value connections in the graph.
Color used for Boolean value connections in the graph.
Color used for Integer value connections in the graph.
Color used for Float value connections in the graph.
Color used for String value connections in the graph.
Color used for StringName value connections in the graph.
Color used for Rect2 value connections in the graph.
Color used for Rect2i value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector2 value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector2i value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector3 value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector3i value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector4 value connections in the graph.
Color used for Vector4i value connections in the graph.
Color used for Transform2D value connections in the graph.
Color used for Transform3D value connections in the graph.
Color used for Plane value connections in the graph.
Color used for Quaternion value connections in the graph.
Color used for AABB value connections in the graph.
Color used for Basis value connections in the graph.
Color used for Projection value connections in the graph.
Color used for Color value connections in the graph.
Color used for NodePath value connections in the graph.
Color used for RID value connections in the graph.
Color used for Object value connections in the graph.
Color used for Dictionary value connections in the graph.
Color used for Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for Callable value connections in the graph.
Color used for Signal value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedByteArray value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedStringArray value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedInt32Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedInt64Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedFloat32Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedFloat64Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedVector2Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedVector3Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedVector4Array value connections in the graph.
Color used for PackedColorArray value connections in the graph.
Default background color of graph nodes.
Default border color of graph nodes.
Border color of selected graph nodes.
Corner radius, in pixels, of graph node borders.
Thickness, in pixels, of graph node borders.
Background color for constant and literal value nodes.
Background color for dialogue nodes.
Background color for event nodes.
Background color for flow-control nodes (branches, loops, and similar).
Background color for engine/native function-call nodes.
Background color for calls to functions defined in this Orchestration.
Background color for calls to functions defined in another script.
Background color for pure (side-effect-free) function-call nodes.
Background color for function entry and exit (terminator) nodes.
Background color for function result nodes.
Background color for math operation nodes.
Background color for memory nodes, such as instantiate and free.
Background color for property get/set nodes.
Background color for resource nodes.
Background color for scene nodes.
Background color for signal nodes.
Background color for variable get/set nodes.
Background color for type-cast nodes.
Background color for comment nodes.
Background tint applied to graph nodes in tool (editor-run) Orchestrations.
